speaker: interviewer
text: The wire-- let's put the wire there. Let's put it here.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. That will be good. At least it won't be in in his hand.

words.text: At least it won't be in

speaker: interviewer
text: I don't know. He'll probably move over, but--

speaker: interviewer
text: Right.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, we can start.

speaker: interviewer
text: Tape 3, Mendel Steinberg. We were talking just before about--

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, the two soldiers was around, and it was the right of way of the horses.

words.text: it was the right of way

speaker: interviewer
text: Right. When you were--

speaker: interviewee
text: And then I was-- I was keeping my head out and looking. That was a small street-- not cars can-- or-- or not autos, and not wagons, too. No horses. The-- the-- the-- When they was--

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes?

speaker: interviewee
text: And they was talking, and I was curious to hear what they are talking. And he was talk-- now there was hearing this talk of Russian. He say, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. That mean, I will say this. But I didn't understand good Russian, I would say. And then I really got excited. And I came out of the hole. It was like a box. Mit-- mit-- doors, should he have been seen. But on the-- on the-- on the hole. I took the box that I made it of, and I say [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] They saw me. Then they took the-- I said [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] I'm a Jew. I'm here with my wife, mit ze child too. And you must-- [? kennst ?] scare. And I didn't know for myself. I was look-- I was looking like a wild man with a long beard. Like-- not like a-- like a wild man.

speaker: interviewer
text: Then they was given, and they came to the hall. And then my [? wife ?] and my child did so. And they stand to crying. And they say I love do you. I say, I am here nine [? mandes. ?] Oh, so they say that they'd go on with I was nine month. Said why you here? You are a [? yavik. ?] I said-- I told him I didn't know this.

speaker: interviewee
text: And after this, I was reminding myself-- I-- I had a story of what I had in a dream. I had a dream one time-- it was rather frightening. I had a dream that the Rabbi from our city-- in a-- in a big Rabbi-- it's-- it's a [? row, ?] a [? rob ?] and a Rabbi. The second one-- and one man, the third one, was a big tall man with a white beard. In the dream, I was thinking-- in the dream-- this must be January. And then I saw him. And he go, and he keep a Sefer Torah, mit a [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] and he say to me, Mendel, dance, dance, until some [INAUDIBLE]. I said, Rabbi, yeah, I would sign [INAUDIBLE], but you tell me then [? degila visan ?] [? Gila ?] then [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] you're coming. And with this, I was-- in dream-- I was to Holland. And then my [? babchen ?] and she was like me-- remember [INAUDIBLE] you know. Then he took 12 and this-- I was operating and I didn't know to 12 days, to 12 weeks, to 12 months-- there was no television. And there, I was counting-- you know-- every day. Exactly 12 months it took with the eight days together. Then they came-- they told me this. They told me, you know, here is a stop-- meaning all the-- the-- the-- all of-- all the-- like the captains, all you guys over there on the tram. Go over there, and they're going to give you everything. We not can come with you, nothing. We are going off to the ocean, to look and to call it in Russian [? asvietkin. ?] That's a Russian. You go looking for-- for-- for the animal, wherever they are. Then I went over there, came to one Polish man, and I said, do you have call him the [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Then he saw me. He said to me, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] you just call me not Mendel, not [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. You know, I was [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. But he said he was a shoemaker. He had a beard-- a black one, like I-- and he was named Moshka.

speaker: interviewer
text: Then I was thinking, I'm not meant-- and not [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Moshka. I say, he say, you know, Moshka-- he say to me, you know, somebody told me one man [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. Do you know [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]? Yes, I said, yes. It's a life. Then I was thinking somebody saw me. No, that was my brother. And I didn't know. I was unknown he killed my brother had a life, and my brother was know-- I got killed. Like I told you, that time where they kill me.

speaker: interviewee
text: That-- that-- that-- the-- over there of the same place where my brother was hiding is a boyfriend used to go to his girl. And he was young about to kill me. And he came-- he didn't know my brother was there. And he said to-- to this man, you know, Mendel [INAUDIBLE] was living till yesterday evening. Yesterday evening, they killed him. [? Lukraine ?] said kill him. I've just lived. And then see then-- then his [? Wendy ?] went to his [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. The number on it was known-- I-- I am not alive. I was not even alive my boss-- was not invited by any is why the name Getz. My brother used to call me Getz-- Used to call me Getz. Getzl-- they used to call him Getz. Polish people, and the Warshawshin people involved with them and get when he was [INAUDIBLE]. You catch him. And you came, he came until they killed Getzl. That was taking my brother. Then I was knowing my brother killed, and he was knowing I am killed. Now, he told me over there are the Jewish people. I begain to go there, straight. I came over there and I went to the window, and he was back in brighter times. And they say-- one girl said, what I was knowing you, growing up together. And so I'm a pair to Getz [INAUDIBLE] [? Sayyid ?] [? Getz ?] [INAUDIBLE]. And I was wondering myself, why'd you say [? Ayid, ?] why you didn't say Mendel [? Aushels? ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: Then I came in the house, and I say to him, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] I say, Getz, you don't recognize me? [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] you don't recognize me? [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Oh, yeah, I say, I'm Mendel [? Aushels. ?] Starting, you know, crying. Then I ask her [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] I told him my son was now dead, and I told my [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] now. He said go right away by-- [INAUDIBLE] No, they told me, you know, your bother is alive, too. Getzl. With your sister. And they told me that, yeah. I said, no. I'm going to see my brother. Then I came to-- I went over there. And I came to my brother. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]

speaker: interviewee
text: When I came in the house, over there it was [? Nuchem ?] [? Tatabo ?] he was from our city. He was a Rabbi. Now, he's a Rabbi in Haifa. He was a Rabbi [INAUDIBLE] now. He was a Rabbi in Haifa. And his brother in law and my sister-in-law and my brother didn't bother. He went to-- to-- to-- to bring the bread. Didn't know I was in the house. And I came. I say, I would Shabbos. And he said [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] When they start and say [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], who you are-- they didn't recognize me. I-- I-- I-- I was wondering. I was too [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] so excited. That [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] I cannot talk one word to you. That they are talking to me, and I couldn't answer them until my brother came. My brother came, and he saw the whole thing. Then he said-- he talked to me. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] then you say to-- I did not-- I didn't answer. Then they say to him, maybe he's a [? stimmer. ?] Maybe he can't talk. No, they said. When he came in the house, he said I would Shabbos. And then my brother started again, talking, talking, talking to me. Why, you know, I didn't answer till-- [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] and I said, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] [SOBBING] [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Then, after this, I told him. He told me-- I-- I know. And he told me I would know. Then, you know, then I told him-- he told me about everything-- how they killed mother, how they killed-- how they killed the sisters. He was know with everything. But I'm the first one-- but I have a long way for to get home.

speaker: interviewer
text: So he told you that the rest of your are family was killed?

speaker: interviewee
text: He told me-- all killed. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] And now, it's happen with me. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] It's happened, a big miracle again. When I came, we-- we was-- we-- I'm afraid to be in a small city. We ran to central [? Wagner. ?] In central [? Wagner ?] was my mother's uncle. He was a big, big [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] He was really to be like a [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] to be a rabbi, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] and I was living near by him. I came over there as Neil Demeschneichin-- they called Demeschneichin. [? Scholynaya ?] [? Ulyasa ?] they was calling them. And they were all the-- well, there was a-- a [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] You see-- but they are taking water here.

speaker: interviewer
text: A well.

speaker: interviewee
text: A well. I went to get my drink. Came one man, and he saw me. And he asked me, are you a Jew? Yes. He said I see you are a Jew. I say to him, I will ask [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] Oh, then you must have known [? Gohara. ?] The name was [? Gohara. ?] I would, but why are you asking, I think. I say, this [? Gohara ?] is my mother's uncle. Oh, sure, nobody ever said we are the neighbors. And then, are you mother, [? Lerner ?] was it your mother's from [INAUDIBLE] Yes. Then I tell you, he say. I have something. [SOB] He gives unto me dreidel. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] He maybe somebody in the Jewish people who are alive-- give this away. No, now, there's no better man than you. You say it's your mother's uncle. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And he brought back a Sefer Torah. [INAUDIBLE] [SOBBING] The same Sefer Torah that I had in [INAUDIBLE]. And the Sefer Torah, I will save him until I brought to Cleveland Ohio. And it was there this was right to buy me a house for the Sefer Torah.

speaker: interviewer
text: My uncle came, and he said Mendel, Rabbi Cohen wants to buy the Sefer Torah. That's a rabbi from the Park Synagogue. He's still alive. And he told me, you must give a lot of speeches-- mention my name. I, the a Jewish man, steal the Torah. And I say, Rabbi Cohen, nobody have such money to pay me for the Sefer Torah. I'm not selling it. And then I became my old, became mad. Who you say did the register to the synagoguge?

speaker: interviewee
text: I say, it can't be registered, that's what they say. You don't have so much money. And you know, when he was giving the speeches, he would say how you eluded me in the speeches. And the Sefer Torah's there now, in Cleveland [? Museum. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: That's the dream that you had about the same thing--

speaker: interviewee
text: That. It's the same dream that I told, the Torah [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. The same-- the same Sefer Torah and gave it to me.

speaker: interviewer
text: Now, when you were in that town with your brother and the rest what was remaining of your family--

speaker: interviewee
text: It was not a town. It was a village.

speaker: interviewer
text: A village. What did you do all do then?

speaker: interviewee
text: See, we came home. We came home [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] I was [INAUDIBLE] And over there were the Russian people. In that time, you know, it was people, but-- but didn't know Russian to talk to them. And this, for me, you know-- I'm talking talking perfect. Then the make me over there the call of Russian as a [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], a manager, of about 48 stores. And I was over there until escape when we was known. It's coming not-- [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] who can come of the-- of the-- of the German like mission. It was about German, but American territory. I'm America was over there. It's when can't get German from over there, we can't even go off Israel. Then we were still the greatest, you know, the border, three months. And I was here to have Sefer Torah with me. All three months I was taking care of [? mit mesel ?] but that's about a miracle. Because I was carrying aboard the Sefer Torah to Cleveland. A Torah by gold and silver. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] I never was believe in my life I'm going to be an American, and I'm going to live in a-- I never will believe in I'll go cart and fill whole bread, a piece of bread. I never in my life-- in that one time I talked with my wife, she will-- she is Fernanda. She's-- my wife is from Czechoslovakia. About She had the same trouble that I had to get in. No US, talk of the US, and know too it was she knows [INAUDIBLE]

speaker: interviewer
text: We was never, never was believing we'd go still with my life, and to be in America, to live in a house like this, and to sleep on a mattress. Who was thinking of this? Nobody was thinking of this. But thank God, now, I am in America and I'm alive. And I'm able to tell my history-- that is not my history. To tell the whole my history, I'd be having to sit down with you all week, and talking day and night, and I did not finish with everything. And I remember everything.

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't remember what the special now-- I have to talk, [INAUDIBLE] with me is now a miracle to. After thems talk, like I told, I was learning the [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] have a boyfriend. And I was reading a lot of poetical poems of anguish. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] And I remember, I can say, twenty [INAUDIBLE] I'm a member of [INAUDIBLE] The newest thing, when I stand Bible-- sometimes I said Bible for people. And I remember. My remember is so still a little good, I remember the Bible. And a matter of fact, I'm not seeing good, and I pray [? bad. ?] Every day I pray, I pray [? bad. ?] And sometimes I feel that I pray for a moment-- pray for [? bad. ?] I have seen it with my own eyes. It's historic. I have a new face for me.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mr. Steinberg, I want to find out and take you back again to when you decided-- what made you decide to come to America? How long were you living in that town?

speaker: interviewee
text: I was in Gemany four years.

speaker: interviewer
text: In Germany?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Germany.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where? [? Liegart ?] [? Rechland ?] I was at logger there for four years. And the [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], they was making and the loggers was a lot of tree-town people. They'd have an accident, you know, something-- all kinds of accident between wide burn and load burn. You know it was all people, but-- then they was making like an [GERMAN], call of German [GERMAN], like our coat. I was the hat, [? finnim ?] coat. I used to be the had and coat. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] And then, I had a big family-- my wife's big family in Cleveland. They sent me papers, affidavits in German. And they warned me in the paper-- in the letters-- should I work myself, I'm a butcher. Why? She had a girlfriend in some Miles factory making salami. He signed an affidavit. He gonna give me a job, started to work [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewee
text: And one uncle, he was a builder-- Abrahams. He told [INAUDIBLE] and myself, I'm a bricklayer. A matter of fact, I had a brother who he died too from cancer here in America. He died already. He came here poor, and he became a millionaire. He left property for $2,500. He became cancer. He died, too. He became a bricklayer, and I became a butcher. I was working one day, and he told me, OK, I could go keep you. You are a good worker. I know, I go, [INAUDIBLE] I'm keeping you.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year did you come to the United States?

speaker: interviewee
text: What?

speaker: interviewer
text: What year did you come to America?

speaker: interviewee
text: What, I became a butcher?

speaker: interviewer
text: No, what year did you come to America?

speaker: interviewee
text: '49. October '49.

speaker: interviewer
text: When--

speaker: interviewee
text: In '49 and then I was working one day, and he told me I'd have to work on Saturday. Then I was back in the-- back in the day [INAUDIBLE] and he said no not help, you want to you go for six days-- five days. Angry, well, who can say five days-- now, not six days-- he say to me, he says, yeah, I know. But you said should I work Thursday. Yeah, Thursday, too. You want should you work all week and one day, Saturday, I should go looking for another man? And they didn't want keeping me, and they send me away. I know they sent me. I said not very much to go away. I mastered all right, and I know I was keeping well the-- just looking for butchers. My son asked where your butchers, you stay kinsman. Well, they are kosher butchers. So anyway they are kinsman. I got [INAUDIBLE] but I was working. And I came over there, then I came to one man, he told me, OK, you want to-- I don't need a butcher. No, you want $25 a week, then you can stand to working. And I decided I go working $20-- I left a job up offering $65, and then I started working for $25.

speaker: interviewer
text: And then the whole family had told me, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] to America. They used to tell me, who is not working Saturday? You want to eat bread, you have to work on Saturday. Then God made-- was showing me a miracle. I was-- I never showed to them a miracle. I didn't want to work on one day, Saturday. And anyway, I became here in America rich. God made me a miracle. God make me a miracle, I'll make for them a miracle-- to show to them. I will show them, I'm not working Saturday, and I can make money without working Saturday, too. Let other people even know this.

speaker: interviewee
text: She got nobody gave how much money you want. You gave how much money you are worth. And you can not work on Saturday, and anyway even became rich, and he became a millionaire, and not working Saturday at all. And matter of fact, I didn't work one hour Saturday. Thanks God, I'm just blessings from God. No I'm was damaged you know from the life of the [INAUDIBLE]. Everyone, but he was [INAUDIBLE] she'd stick too. she-- she will try this. You know, you-- she'd do it. She had this. Everyone would come, think when [INAUDIBLE] comes, you gave an [INAUDIBLE], you can't be everything. Everyone is sick. And yet, you are paying to now read the [INAUDIBLE] when you called everything by then you get a thousand [INAUDIBLE] It's the means of dominating. Now, my wife taught my brother for me, you stand to pay now for the-- for the people. You know, for the dead people. My brother called me up-- Mendel, so you know, I'm the one taking for the [? blood ?] for my mother. [PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS] [SOBBING] For my sister's bonus money, anymore-- we came to the [INAUDIBLE] don't take two. And I didn't took nothing. And I ran the [? blood ?] taking, should-- should-- should the German who is paying the money, she was taking-- oh, I pay over all year. Good for that, a payoff. I did not took nothing. And I don't want nothing for that.

speaker: interviewer
text: When you came to America, you lived in what city?

speaker: interviewee
text: Straight, I came to Cleveland. And I see as I came to Cleveland. And like I say, [? full stink ?] when-- when-- when-- when the wealth and fin them Salami maker, for Miles. I was-- somebody sent me to Brooklyn in the lumber-- lumber yard. I was working one year in a lumber yard. No, over there it was the manager Mr Kaiser. He was a Jew from Holland. You know, educated Jew. And you-- when we used to, you know, make lunch, he used to come. He used to like talking to me. Two year I is, but-- but I bet he really now, you say, I am educated man. One time I was can't find where I have a material. And I was in it, I sweared, and you know it was a-- then he came in looking for you.

speaker: interviewer
text: He say to me, Mr. Steinberg, you are such an educated man. Why you have to work on so hard? I say, Herr Kaiser, I came here. I brought a wife and two children. I must make a living. I am not this kind of people who they came here, should-- should-- they should [INAUDIBLE] people should they be seen taking charity. That's what I must do-- sometimes then maybe God helped me.

speaker: interviewee
text: Then he'd call them, and the money man, he said this Herr Steinberg don't sign any more doing the outwork. He told me to put in their shop, to put-- to-- pulling boats to drive up. It was light work. And I was working over there almost a year. And then the business agent, he told me when you go I have a job for me, the butcher business agent. He was going to put me one a butcher shop. In a butcher--

speaker: interviewer
text: We'll stop this part here, and we'll talk about the butcher on the next tape.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK.
